metadata.name: pysmelt
metadata.summary: smelt is a system to describe, run, and analyze integration tests

functions.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.path_utils.relatavize_inp_path
documentation: This function converts the input path, which is relative to the current working directory to be relative to `abs_path`

modules.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.executed_tests
documentation: Generated by the protocol buffer compiler.  DO NOT EDIT!
sources: executed_tests.proto
plugin: python-betterproto

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.executed_tests.Digest
documentation: Taken directly from the bazel reapi, for compat

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.executed_tests.TestResult
documentation:
The serialized record of single test execution A backend that stores this
data should normalize the data in this object into a "TestDef" and a
"TestInstantiation". I don't think it's complexity that should be exposed
to the frontend

variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.command.CStatusStr
documentation: ignore

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.command.Command

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.command.Command.script
documentation: A list of bash commands that will be executed in sequence

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.command.Command.dependent_files
documentation:
Paths to the files that are expected to be created by this command 

Anything here will be treated as an "artifact"

The simplest unit of compute in smelt -- commands are the nodes that are scheduled and executed by the runtime

Functionally, Command is a simple wrapper around a `bash` script

functions.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.analysis.most_recent_invoke_path
documentation: By default, we push the most recent invocation to this path

functions.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.analysis.most_recent_junit_path
documentation: By default, we push the most recent invocation to this path

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.analysis.IQL
documentation: This is the "Invocation Query Layer" -- a helper class to get data out from Invocations

variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.target.NamedFiles
documentation:
Named files are a collection of names mapping to SmeltPaths

We use str instead of the SmeltPath in the value to keep implimentation simple

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.target.Target

methods.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.target.Target.get_dependencies
documentation: Returns the targets that this target depends on

methods.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.target.Target.get_dependent_files
documentation: Returns the files that this target depends on

methods.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.target.Target.as_ref
documentation: Currently refs are just the names of each target

A target is a structure that holds logic to generate a `Command`

Targets are higher level abstraction to commands -- they allow users to embed "application logic" into targets

functions.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.paths.local_smelt_path_fetcher
documentation: The default SmeltPathFetcher, which fetches the smelt path from the local filesystem.

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.interfaces.paths.SmeltFilePath
documentation:
A file object in smelt can either be absolute or defined relative to the command_def path

for instance, if we have an output "foo"

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.default_targets.raw_bash
py-smelt.pysmelt.default_targets.test_group
documentation: Target for oragnizing tests

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.rc.SmeltRC.smelt_root
documentation: Path to smelt root -- is $GIT_ROOT by default

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.rc.SmeltRC.smelt_rules_dir
documentation: Directory that holds third party smelt rules

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.rc.SmeltRC.jobs
documentation: Number of job slots that can be used

modules.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.executed_tests
documentation: Generated by the protocol buffer compiler.  DO NOT EDIT!
sources: executed_tests.proto
plugin: python-betterproto
This file has been @generated

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.executed_tests.Digest

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.executed_tests.Digest.hash
documentation:
The hash. In the case of SHA-256, it will always be a lowercase hex string
exactly 64 characters long. in expectation, this should always be the
sha256

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.executed_tests.Digest.size_bytes
documentation: The size of the blob, in bytes.
Taken directly from the bazel reapi, for compat

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.executed_tests.TestResult

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.executed_tests.TestResult.test_name
documentation: name of the test

The serialized record of single test execution This is the api that we
should build tools around -- if people provide tracked tests

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.executed_tests.TestOutputs.artifacts
documentation: Files that are expected from a test

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.executed_tests.TestOutputs.exit_code
documentation: exit code of the test

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.executed_tests.Invocation
documentation:
Highest level invocation for a set of tests -- must contain one or more
test results any time we run any test(s), an invocation object is created

functions.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.output_utils.pretty_print_tests
documentation: pretty print all the tests in the graph

documentation: only memory and cpu

documentation:
Eventually, perhaps we should encode this as info in protobuf not today
babey

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ClientResp
documentation: Responses to the client command

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ConfigureSmelt

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ConfigureSmelt.smelt_root
documentation: Should be an absolute path

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ConfigureSmelt.job_slots
documentation: number of slots the entire executor has -- analogous to job slots in make

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ConfigureSmelt.prof_cfg
documentation: configures how we profile commands

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ConfigureSmelt.test_only
documentation: If true, we ignore the non test commands

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ConfigureSmelt.silent
documentation: If true, we do not transmit stdout from the server

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ConfigureSmelt.prepare_workspace
documentation:
if true, smelt execution will prepare the workspace to run tests in another
stage this is useful for splitting the work between a build and test stage

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ConfigureSmelt.sandbox_env
documentation: if true, all commands will have their environment variables sandboxed

This configuration is done once, when SMELT is initialized The client
should provide this when creating an smelt handle

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ProfilerCfg.prof_type
documentation: if we enable simple profiling

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ServerInfo

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ServerInfo.hostname
documentation: this is the hostname used by smelt and all workers, by default

class_variables.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.ServerInfo.port
documentation:
This is port supplied to each worker a. Sometimes you'll have a proxy in
front of your smelt server to make deployment easier. e.g.  this is
practical for  deploying in non-LAN networking environments

Populate info for the slurm server  This is configured from the end-user
side.  if these data is not provided, then smelt will execute `whoami` to
derive hostname and will let the OS select the port

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.AwsCreds
documentation: credentials required to create an S3 Client and upload artifacts to it

classes.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.proto.smelt_client.commands.Ulimit
documentation: Clone of Bollard proto message Ulimit

functions.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.generators.procedural.capture_targets
documentation:
Patch the __post_init__ method of `Target` to append new instances of
Target to the yielded list.

functions.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.pygraph.spin_for_message
documentation: Utility for spinning for a message

methods.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.pygraph.PyGraph.console_runloop
documentation:
Generator that will try to consume as many `Event` messages as possible

All of the stdout from the "test_name" command will be given to the `sink`.
By default, sink should just be print

The yielded value will be

methods.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.pygraph.PyGraph.run_one_test_interactive
documentation:
Runs a single test, with a "sink" handle to process all of the stdout for that specific command

By default, this will just print stdout + stderr to the screen -- it looks like you're running the command interactively
self.retcode_tracker.reset()

methods.name: py-smelt.pysmelt.pygraph.PyGraph.set_commands
documentation:
Initializes the list of commands that are visible to the smelt runtime


If the list of commands are malformed -- e.g. syntax error in the yaml, then an error will be thrown

documentation: holds all of the commands that are live in the graph

documentation: Handle to rust -- calls the function defined in the pyo3 bindings
